key_components: BlobStorageService: Content-addressed blob storage with streaming SHA-256
extractImageMetadata: Image processing with EXIF stripping
detectMimeType: Magic-byte based MIME detection with sharp fallback

AC-008.evidence: computeStreamingSHA256 uses Node.js crypto.createHash with streaming
AC-008.test_coverage: BlobStorageService.test.ts: Streaming SHA-256 Hashing suite (7 tests)

AC-009.evidence: Blobs stored at {blobsDir}/{hash} with hash as primary key

AC-010.evidence: extractImageMetadata extracts MIME, size, dimensions, blurhash
AC-010.test_coverage: BlobStorageService.test.ts: Image Metadata Extraction suite (3 tests)

AC-011.evidence: storeBlob checks for existing hash before storing, returns deduplicated=true

AC-033.evidence: Files stored in {blobsDir}/{hash} with no extension

AC-041.evidence: sharp.rotate() strips EXIF automatically during processing

AC-048.evidence: computeStreamingSHA256 uses createReadStream with incremental hash updates
AC-048.test_coverage: BlobStorageService.test.ts: Streaming SHA-256 Hashing suite, large file test

technical_notes.streaming_implementation: Uses Node.js crypto.createHash('sha256') with fs.createReadStream, processes chunks incrementally without buffering entire file
technical_notes.exif_stripping: Sharp's rotate() method auto-orients and strips all EXIF metadata. Non-images bypass this processing.
technical_notes.mime_detection: Uses file-type library (magic bytes) with sharp metadata fallback for images
technical_notes.database_access: Uses raw sql.js Database API (not Knex) following existing patterns from NostlingService
technical_notes.deduplication: Content-addressed storage means identical files naturally deduplicate - hash collision = same file
technical_notes.security: Files stored with mode 0o600, directory with mode 0o700 (user-only access)

results.evidence: Property tests bypass streaming impl, use crypto directly. No varying file size tests via computeStreamingSHA256.
No tests simulate EXIF stripping failures. Success paths only. No error propagation verification.
Streaming createReadStream with hash.update chunks confirmed.
Proper getDatabase() pattern, sql.js API, parameterized queries.
Concrete and property-based dedup tests, deduplicated flag verified.
5 test cases cover image/non-image metadata distinction.
Directory 0o700, blob files 0o600 with tests.
AC-048 streaming SHA-256 fully satisfied.
All functions/imports used, no dead code.
No duplication with ImageCacheService; different purposes.
Integration tests cover store→retrieve→delete lifecycle plus image processing.
New property test using service.storeBlob() with fc.uint8Array varying sizes (0-100KB), verifies streaming hash matches expected.
Two sharp mock failure tests: rotate() failure and metadata() failure, both verify 'Failed to process image' error.

patterns.pattern_description: Property tests tested crypto module directly rather than streaming implementation; EXIF failure paths had no test coverage

issues_addressed.issue: Property tests bypass computeStreamingSHA256, test crypto module directly
issues_addressed.action_taken: Added property-based test using fc.uint8Array with varying sizes (0-100KB), calls service.storeBlob() which exercises computeStreamingSHA256, verifies hash matches expected
issues_addressed.evidence: New test at ~line 159-172, 15 iterations with varying sizes

issues_addressed.issue: No EXIF stripping failure scenario tests
issues_addressed.action_taken: Added 2 tests: (1) sharp.rotate() failure mock → storeBlob rejects with 'Failed to process image', (2) sharp.metadata() failure mock → storeBlob rejects with 'Failed to process image'
